best_edges.edge: VALID_0DTE pullback continuation remains the main realized PnL engine.
best_edges.evidence: pullback_long generated $2566.4 over 33 trades, 239.87 PnL/exposure hour, 100% win rate, no slow >=12h trades, and worst MAE -403.2. It is high heat but still the largest contribution.

best_edges.edge: Charm-pin pullback has fast-turnover edge despite owning the global worst tail.
best_edges.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long generated $1474.1 over 38 trades, median hold 5.3 minutes, average MAE -71.6, and 21.1% of total PnL. The problem is one -527 MAE trade, not broad profile failure.

best_edges.edge: 1DTE breakout fallback is profitable and should not be bluntly tightened again.
best_edges.evidence: breakout_long_1dte produced $1310.2 over 20 trades with no slow trades. Prior direct 1DTE quality tightening hurt objective by -355 to -594 score and worsened worst MAE in tests.

best_edges.edge: Squeeze continuation is clean enough to keep stable while risk-reducing elsewhere.
best_edges.evidence: squeeze_long produced $721 over 10 trades, worst MAE -208.25, no slow trades, and prior squeeze upside probes were accepted or flat without solving the current tail problem.

broken_edges.action: avoid broad pruning; use surgical veto/routing research only
broken_edges.evidence: Worst MAE is concentrated in charm_pin_pullback_long at -527 with a high ES-VWAP extension, weak RSI/ADX, delayed profit path, and existing veto warnings. Single setup floor >=62 would retain only 40.4% PnL, too destructive.
broken_edges.problem: Charm-pin tail is real but not separable by a broad scalar threshold.

broken_edges.evidence: Prior 1DTE gates on structure, VWAP slope, forward DEX, and TP cap reduced net profit by $317-$558 and did not improve risk-adjusted score.
broken_edges.problem: 1DTE average MAE is high, but direct quality filters cannibalize profitable trades.

broken_edges.evidence: charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 3 latest trades, $446.2 PnL, 462 PnL/exposure hour, but the latest exit-compression experiment lost $146.7 and -143.6 score with no worst-MAE improvement.
broken_edges.problem: Small-sample convex payoff is too easy to damage with TP compression.

broken_edges.action: do not optimize on tiny stale fallback samples in same batch
broken_edges.evidence: STALE/EXPIRED no-0DTE states total only 6 trades and $370.2 PnL. Stale breakout fallback has weak efficiency, but sample size is too small for mixed global edits.
broken_edges.problem: Fallback risk evidence is underpowered and can distract from the VALID_0DTE tail.

market_regime_read: Strong long-only momentum/pullback tape with unusually clean realized exits: 111 trades, $6979.1 PnL, 100% realized win rate, 0.4% drawdown, zero slow >=12h trades. Risk is hidden intratrade heat, not realized losses. The next research should reduce tail MAE from -527 and average MAE near -101 while preserving the high-throughput pullback/charm/squeeze edges.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Use early path behavior to distinguish recoverable charm-pin extension from trap entries.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Requires code because current config sees entry state only; the worst charm-pin trade had no positive progress before MAE and first +1.5 points only after 80 minutes, while protected extended-VWAP winners often resolve quickly.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path timing, first 15m MFE/MAE, positive-before-MAE flag, profile, ES-VWAP extension, RSI/ADX slopes.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long with high ES-VWAP extension or recent-high rejection plus weak early progress.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE from -527 toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL and >=95% protected fast-winner count.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Split 1DTE fallback into breakout continuation versus pullback/reclaim behavior instead of tightening breakout_long_1dte directly.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May be unreachable or cannibalize profitable breakout_long_1dte if router ordering overlaps too broadly.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: 1DTE option map state, ES reclaim/pullback features, VWAP distance/slope, structure score, forward DEX.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_1DTE_ONLY when ES state is pullback/reclaim rather than breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1310, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.9, and total net profit remains >=98% of leader.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Route charm/pin avoidance states into dormant profiles to isolate tail-heavy pin risk without harming normal charm-pin winners.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Dormant activation may capture clean winners rather than only tail-risk states.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Dormant Activation
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Existing dormant charm_wall_pin_avoid_long or late_day_pin_avoid_long profile, router activation, charm wall/pin proximity, ES-VWAP extension, pullback-from-high state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE high pin/charm-wall risk currently routed into charm_pin_pullback_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile positive standalone or combined charm/pin worst MAE improves, total net profit >=98% of leader, completed trades down <=5.

next_experiments.config_bias: Bias toward a tiny expansion of the already-enabled extended-VWAP-rejection veto boundaries that missed the six worst rows by one feature at a time. Avoid broad ES-VWAP max-distance or setup-score floors because protected extended-VWAP winners contribute $715.7 with median MAE -15.3.
next_experiments.goal: Catch near-miss deep-MAE rows while preserving extended-VWAP fast winners.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The current extended-VWAP rejection veto is close to the right tail shape; small boundary nudges can reduce worst MAE with less PnL loss than new global filters.
next_experiments.name: Extended VWAP Rejection Boundary Retest
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE improves from -527 toward <= -403 with total PnL retained >=97%, completed trades down <=5, and protected extended-VWAP winner retention >=90%.
next_experiments.risk: Over-widening RSI-slope or pullback bounds may block many profitable extension entries.

next_experiments.config_bias: Focus only on charm_pin_pullback_long tail states with high ES-VWAP extension, large rolling-high/pullback distance, weak RSI slope, and ADX near or below 18. Do not add a global setup floor or disable the profile.
next_experiments.goal: Remove or down-rank the -527 charm-pin tail without damaging the fast charm-pin book.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst tail is a narrow high-extension charm-pin rejection state, not the normal charm-pin pullback edge.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin High-Extension Tail Box
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE <= -403, profile PnL retained >=90%, total PnL retained >=98%, fast_under_15m trades down <=3.
next_experiments.risk: The box may overlap profitable high-extension charm-pin winners if thresholds are too broad.

next_experiments.config_bias: Research a mild entry-veto consequence escalation for entries already accumulating 4-5 warning points and poor structure or delayed-profit fingerprints. Keep it scoped to tail-heavy profiles rather than all profiles.
next_experiments.goal: Convert repeated warning clusters into fewer deep-heat entries without starving the edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Several worst rows were allowed despite existing veto reasons; making high-warning clusters more consequential may reduce MAE better than raw indicator tightening.
next_experiments.name: High Veto-Points Consequence Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Average MAE improves by >=5%, worst MAE does not worsen, total PnL retained >=97%, and no increase in slow trades.
next_experiments.risk: Entry-veto points are common among winners; broad escalation can remove high-PnL trades.

next_experiments.config_bias: Study pullback_long weak-structure tails separately from charm-pin. Prefer profile-local structure/VWAP/reclaim balance, not a global structure score increase, because structure-score buckets are broadly profitable.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce pullback_long average MAE and the -403 tail while preserving its dominant PnL contribution.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Pullback_long is profitable enough to tolerate a small local risk trim if the optimizer targets weak structure plus poor VWAP/reclaim context, not structure alone.
next_experiments.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Local Trim
next_experiments.primary_metric: pullback_long worst MAE improves from -403.2, average MAE improves versus -113.2, pullback PnL retained >=92%, total PnL retained >=98%.
next_experiments.risk: Pullback weak-structure trades are also a large profit bucket; blunt pruning will damage the core edge.

next_experiments.config_bias: Freeze direct breakout_long_1dte tightening and instead only test a companion split if the optimizer can activate pullback_long_1dte through the 1DTE router. Avoid repeating structure/slope/TP-cap gates on breakout_long_1dte.
next_experiments.goal: Improve 1DTE risk by routing pullback-like 1DTE states rather than weakening the breakout fallback.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Prior 1DTE tightening failed because it pruned profitable breakout entries; a dedicated 1DTE pullback companion may separate regimes better.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Split Feasibility
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= current $1310.2, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.9, total net profit >=98% of leader.
next_experiments.risk: Router overlap may simply move trades without improving risk, or the dormant companion may be unreachable.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not tune squeeze_long or charm_wall_breakaway_long in the next risk-reducer batch except as holdout controls. Recent squeeze changes were flat/accepted but not risk-reducing; charm-wall compression hurt objective.
next_experiments.goal: Prevent optimizer churn in clean or small-sample profiles while tail research runs.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The best near-term score improvement comes from tail containment in charm/pullback profiles, not further TP or throughput changes in clean profiles.
next_experiments.name: Clean-Edge Holdout Control
next_experiments.primary_metric: No regression in total PnL, completed trades, or PnL/exposure while tail experiments are evaluated.
next_experiments.risk: Freezing these profiles may miss upside, but the selected track is risk-reducer.

research_notes: Run_023 is slightly below the leader in score and net profit but has better exposure efficiency. The dominant research target is still hidden MAE: worst -527, average near -101, and bad rows largely covered by an extended-VWAP rejection near-miss family. Because every trade is a winner, risk reduction must be judged by retained PnL, protected fast winners, and exposure quality, not win rate. Avoid repeating experiments already shown harmful: direct 1DTE tightening, call-wall broad loosening, pullback TP/max-distance re-expansion, and charm-wall exit compression.
risk_controls: Reject any candidate that retains <97% total PnL unless worst MAE improves materially below -403 and trade loss is <=5.
Protect fast_winners and extended_vwap_winners; do not accept broad filters that block these fingerprints without clear tail improvement.
Keep completed-trade loss <=10% absolute batch rule, with tighter <=5 trade target for risk-reducer experiments.
Do not enable runtime health exits or hard adverse stops in this plan; prior runtime-risk validation improved MAE but destroyed objective score.
Treat dormant profile activation as isolated discovery, not mixed with global tail-veto edits.
